content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach handled the safety disclosure well but the dimension being scored is reads — naming how the client is showing up somatically, energetically, or linguistically. The coach does name one important linguistic/relational read in turn 8 ('I'm not going to forget it. You said something important') — catching and refusing the client's retraction attempt ('Forget I said that') is a real read of the defensive walk-back. That's a meaningful, timely read at a pivotal moment. However, other obvious reads were missed: the rush-to-disclaim-and-retract signature in turn 7, the 'I've done therapy, I've meditated, I've journaled' list-as-armor in turn 5, the somatic flatness itself never named as a state ('I notice how flat your voice is as you say that'), and 'I'm just tired' in turn 9 went un-read. Early turns (2, 4, 6) are content-level questions with no observational language grounding them. One specific, well-timed read plus generic warmth, with multiple missed read opportunities, places this solidly in the 2 band.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Content-level question about the flatness; no read named."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Blind 'what's underneath' question with no observation grounding it; misses the list-of-tried-things armor in turn 3/5."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Borrowed Joe question delivered without any read of how the client is showing up; treats turn 5's defended list as ordinary material."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches and names the retraction ('I'm not going to forget it. You said something important') — a real read of the defensive walk-back at the exact moment it occurs."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Strong safety move but operates at content level; no somatic or energetic read named."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Misses the read on 'I just haven't wanted to worry her' (the protective/isolating pattern) — engages it logistically rather than naming it."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "The rush signature was active in turn 7 (disclosure-then-immediate-retraction-then-minimization in three sentences) and went unnamed as such, though the coach did refuse the retraction functionally."
}
